stories.title: Glimpse became Baxtori, with a real GitHub boundary.
stories.brief: The prototype now signs in through a private GitHub App, limits private-code access to chosen repositories, and keeps credentials in an encrypted server session.
stories.verdict: A meaningful product boundary worth understanding
stories.whatChanged: The public repository fallback was replaced by GitHub App authorization, authenticated repository APIs, encrypted HttpOnly sessions, and account-scoped reading state.
stories.whyItMatters: Repository visibility now follows the signed-in GitHub account instead of demo data. The interface and the server enforce the same access boundary.
stories.verify: Sign out, sign back in, and confirm the private glimpse repository appears while an ungranted private repository does not.
stories.tradeoff: The GitHub session currently lives in a cookie, which is excellent for interactive use but cannot power unattended reviews by itself; the scheduled Codex job supplies that missing execution context.
stories.evidence: 1 squash merge · 18 files · auth and repository API tests

codeEvidence.title: Encrypt the server session
codeEvidence.why: The GitHub token is serialized only inside an AES-GCM-encrypted session value. A fresh IV is generated for every seal, and malformed or undecryptable cookies fail closed.

codeEvidence.title: Use the token server-side
codeEvidence.why: Repository access begins by resolving the encrypted session in the route handler. The access token is attached to GitHub's server-to-server request and is never included in the JSON sent to the browser.

codeEvidence.title: Return identity, not credentials
codeEvidence.why: The browser-facing status endpoint exposes only configuration state and the signed-in user's profile. The GitHub access token remains behind the route boundary.
